sections.paragraph:
fut. יָקוּם apoc. יָקֹףּ, וַיָּֽקָם, pret. once in the Arabic manner, קָאם Hos 10:14

(1) to arise (Arab. فَامَ, Syr. ܩܳܡ id.), from a seat, from bed, Gen 19:1 23:3 Lev 19:32, etc. Sometimes with the verbosity common in such cases amongst the Orientals, it is pleon. prefixed to verbs of going, going forward, and of setting about any thing with impulse, Gen 22:3, וַיָּֽקָם וַיֵּלֶךְ “he arose and went”. Job 1:20, “he arose and rent his mantle.” 2Sa 13:31 1Sa 24:5 (see Schult. on Job, loc. cit.). Imp. קוּמָה Arise! often used as a word of incitement, especially to Jehovah that he may grant aid, Num 10:35 Psa 3:8 7:7 9:20 17:13 , compare Psal. 68:2 with a dative pleon. קוּמִי לָךְ Son 2:10. Specially it is-(a) to arise against any one, followed by עַל Psa 3:2 54:5 86:14 Isai. 31:2 אֶל Gen 4:8 also to rise as a witness against any one, followed by בְּ Psa 27:12 Job 16:8 (compare עָנָה בְ). In the participle with suffixes, as קָמַי those who rise up against me, Psa 18:40 קָמָיו Deu 33:11. Comp. קִים.-(b) to exist, to go forth, used of the light, Job 25:3 of a star, Numb. 24:17 of life as compared with noonday, Job 11:17 of the birth of a king or prophet (auftreten), Exo 1:8 Deu 34:10 of future time (aufkommen), Gen 41:30.-(c) to grow up, to become a man, spoken of a youth, Psa 78:6 hence to increase with riches, to flourish, Pro 28:12.

(2) to stand, i.q. עָמַד No. 1, 2.-(a) followed by לִפְנֵי to stand before any one, to oppose him, Jos 7:13.-(b) to stand fast (beftehn), to remain, to continue, Job 15:29 Amo 7:2, 1Sa 24:21 13:14 (compare תְּקוּמָה); followed by לְ to remain to any one, Lev 25:30 followed by עַל to persevere in any thing (auf etwas beftehn), Isa 32:8.-(c) to be confirmed; of a purchase, Gen 23:17, 20 Gen 23:20of a counsel or purpose, Isa 8:10 14:24 Pro 19:21 (once followed by לְ to be successful to any one, Job 22:28); of a prediction, i.q. בֹּוא No. 2, let. e, Jerem. 44:28 , 29 Jer 44:29opp. to נָפַל No. 1, let. h; to be valid, to stand good, e.g. of testimony, Deu 19:15 a vow, Num 30:5, seq.-(d) to stand by for aid to any one; followed by לְ Psa 94:16 (Arab. قام seq., ل id.).-(e) קָמוּ עֵינַיִם 1Ki 14:4 compare 1Sa 4:15 the eyes stand; spoken of a blind person suffering from amaurosis, the pupil of whose eye is set, and does not contract with the light of the sun.

(3) like the Sam. to live. See Piel No. 2, and the noun יְקוּם .

Piel קִיֵּם (principally in the later books; like the Aram. קַיֵּם, ܩܰܝܡܶ)-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 2; in various connections-(a) to make valid, to confirm, Rth 4:7 Est 9:29, 31 , init.; to confirm a prediction by the event, Eze 13:6.-(b) followed by עַל to injoin any thing on any one; pr. to cause any thing to be imposed upon any one (compare Chald. קַיֵּם עַל to bind any one by an oath), Est 9:21, 31 , med. Hence קִיֵּם עָלָיו to take upon one-self; pr. to impose upon oneself, Est 9:27, 31 , fin.-(c) to fulfil, to perform (an oath), Psa 119:106.

( Psa 119:2) trans. of Kal No. 3; to preserve alive, Psa 119:28 (more frequently in Targg.).

Pilel קֹומֵם-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 1, to raise up, to build up, e.g. ruins, Isa 44:26 58:12 61:4 .

(2) intrans. to rise up. Mic 2:8, “long ago has my people לְאֹיֵב יְקֹומֵם risen up as an enemy.” Vulg. consurrexit. Others take it, “long ago has my people set (sc. me) up as an enemy (to themselves).”

Hiphil הֵקִים-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 1, to cause to arise-(a) to erect, raise up one fallen down, Deu 22:4 the afflicted, Job 4:4 Psa 41:11.-(b) to set up, e.g. a tent, Exo 26:30 a statue, Deu 16:22 an altar, 1Ki 16:32 towers, Isa 23:13 also, to set up again a tent fallen down, Amo 9:11 hence הֵקִים אֶרֶץ, הֵקִים שִׁבְטֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל to reset up the land, the tribes of Israel, i.e. to restore them, Isa 49:6, Isa 49:8. הֵקִים בְּרִית to make a covenant (einen Bund errichten), Gen 6:18 9:11 17:7 .-(c) to cause to come forth or to exist, to raise up any one, as judges, Jud. 2:18 a prophet, Jer 29:15 a priest, 1Sa 2:35 an enemy, Mic 5:4. Specially, לְ הֵקִים שֵׁם Deu 25:7 Rth 4:5, 10 and הֵקִים זֶרַע לְ Gen 38:8, to raise up to any one a name, seed, or posterity, i.e. to raise up, by marrying his widow, children for him who shall bear his name.

(2) to cause to stand, Psa 40:3 (a) to constitute any one king, Deu 28:36.-(b) to cause to stand still, to restrain, Psa 107:29.-(c) to confirm, to establish anything, Num 30:14, 15 Num 30:15to perform a promise, 1Sa 1:23 an oath, Gen 26:3.

Hophal הוּקַם-

(1) to be set up, erected, Exo 40:17.

(2) to be constituted, 2Sa 23:1.

(3) to be confirmed, Jer 35:14.

Hithpael הִתְקֹומֵם to rise up with a hostile mind, Psa 17:7 followed by לְ against any one, Job 20:27. Part. with suff. מִתְקֹומְמִי my adversary, Psa 59:2 Job 27:7.

Derivatives, קֹומָה, קֹומְמִיּוּת, יְקוּם, מָקֹום, קִים, קִימָה, קָמָה, תְּקוּמָה, תְּקֹומֵם, and the pr.n. יָקִים, קָמֹון.

קום

Chald.-

(1) to arise, Dan 3:24 to come forth, to exist, Dan 2:39 7:17 .

(2) to stand, Dan 3:3 7:17 also, to endure, to remain, Dan 2:44.

Pael, קַיֵּם to establish. קַיֵּם קְיָם to establish a statute, to give forth a mandate, Dan 6:8.

Aphel הֲקֵים, once אֲקֵים Dan 3:1. Pl. הֲקִימוּ, part. מְהָקֵים, fut. יְקִים and יְהָקֵים

(1) to erect, to set up, e.g. a statue, Dan 3:1, seq.

(2) to constitute, to appoint (a king) [a priest], Ezr 6:18 followed by עַל to set over, Dan 4:14 6:2 .

Hophal הֳקִים (in the Hebrew manner), to stand, to be made to stand, Dan 7:4.

Derivatives, קְיָם, קַיָּם.
